name: PAN / TAN Registration
description: PAN of Indian entity — required for customs registration and GST. Foreign companies need an Indian PAN and typically a local agent or project office. TAN (Tax Deduction Account Number) also needed for TDS compliance on certain payments.

name: Importer Exporter Code Registration
description: Obtain IEC from DGFT (Directorate General of Foreign Trade). This is the primary registration required for any entity importing goods into India. Apply online at dgft.gov.in.

name: GST Registration
description: Register for Goods and Services Tax with CBIC. Required for claiming input tax credit on IGST paid at customs. Register at gst.gov.in.

name: ICEGATE Registration
description: Register on Indian Customs Electronic Gateway (ICEGATE) for electronic filing of Bills of Entry, shipping bills, and customs documents. Register at icegate.gov.in.

name: Authorised Dealer Bank Designation
description: Designate an Authorised Dealer (AD) bank for foreign exchange transactions related to import payments. The AD bank handles forex remittances and ensures FEMA compliance.

name: Appoint Customs House Agent (CHA)
description: Engage a licensed Customs House Agent (also called Custom Broker) to handle customs clearance at the port of entry. CHA files Bill of Entry, handles inspections, and manages clearance.
timeline: Can be done anytime before shipment arrival

name: Marine Insurance
description: Obtain marine cargo insurance covering the goods in transit. Required for CIF valuation and to protect against loss or damage during shipping.

name: File Bill of Entry
description: File Bill of Entry through ICEGATE before or upon arrival of goods. This is the primary customs declaration document for imports. Can be filed as Home Consumption (white) or Warehousing (yellow).
timeline: Can be filed up to 30 days before vessel arrival

name: Pay Customs Duty
description: Pay assessed customs duties (BCD, IGST, SWS, Compensation Cess) through electronic payment via ICEGATE. Duties must be paid before goods can be cleared.

name: Customs Examination and Clearance
description: Goods examined by customs officer (physical or document-based via RMS - Risk Management System). First Check or Second Check examination. Out of Charge order issued upon satisfactory examination.
